mission.title: rook: ceph: support for S3 buckets as DNS subdomain style
mission.description: ceph: support for S3 buckets as DNS subdomain style. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current rook deployment
steps.description:
Verify your rook version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
helm list -n rook 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working rook installation.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
**Is this a bug report or feature request?**
* Feature Request

**What should the feature do:**
Currently only the bucket Path style is supported.

steps.title: Apply the fix for ceph: support for S3 buckets as DNS subdomain style
steps.description:
Another workaround suggested in Slack channel. It works in my cluster! We need to modify zonegroup in the toolbox container.
```
[rook]$ radosgw-admin zonegroup get > zonegroup.json
[rook]$ vi  zonegroup.json
...
    "hostnames": ["SERVICE_NAME.rook-ceph.svc","YOUR DOMAIN FOR SUB DOMAIN STYLE"],
  
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: rook-config-override
  namespace: rook-ceph
data:
  config: |
    [global]
    rgw dns name = files.example.com
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "ceph: support for S3 buckets as DNS subdomain style" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Another workaround suggested in Slack channel. It works in my cluster! We need to modify zonegroup in the toolbox container.
```
[rook]$ radosgw-admin zonegroup get > zonegroup.json
[rook]$ vi  zonegroup.json
...
    "hostnames": ["SERVICE_NAME.rook-ceph.svc","YOUR DOMAIN FOR SUB DOMAIN STYLE"],
    "hostnames_s3website": ["..."],
...
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: rook-config-override
  namespace: rook-ceph
data:
  config: |
    [global]
    rgw dns name = files.example.com
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it $(kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod -l "app=rook-ceph-tools" -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') bash

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with rook installed or the issue environment reproducible.
